explanations.22: React.isValidElement() checks if something is actually a React component (not just a plain object or string).
explanations.26: The regex /language-(\w+)/ looks for the word 'language-' followed by letters/numbers, and match[1] grabs just the part after 'language-'.
explanations.29: The ?. operator (optional chaining) safely accesses node.props.children even if node.props doesn't exist — it just returns undefined instead of crashing.
explanations.31: This checks if nested is an array so we can loop through multiple children; otherwise we treat it as a single child.
explanations.273: useMemo wraps code that shouldn't re-run every render — here we split the code into lines and syntax-highlight them only when code or lang changes.
explanations.279: If Prism doesn't have a syntax highlighter for the language, we fall back to plaintext or a generic 'clike' grammar so highlighting doesn't break.
explanations.288: We remove extra lines from the highlighted output to match the number of raw lines, since syntax highlighting sometimes adds or removes content.
explanations.293: This toggle function switches activeLine between the line number and null — clicking the same line twice turns the explanation off.
explanations.311: dangerouslySetInnerHTML tells React 'I know this is risky, but I'm putting safe HTML here' — Prism's output is safe because it only wraps code in span tags.
explanations.318: These mouse events make the tooltip appear on hover; onMouseLeave hides it again when you move the mouse away.
explanations.367: useMemo parses the eli5json string (which comes as JSON text) into a JavaScript object, and caches it so we don't re-parse it every render.
explanations.383: useId() generates a unique string ID that persists across renders, so the button's aria-controls always matches the content div's id.
explanations.385: This useMemo tries three ways to find the language: from a prop, from HTML children, or from the filename extension.
explanations.395: filename.split('.').pop() splits the filename by dots and takes the last piece — so 'app.tsx' becomes 'tsx'.
explanations.404: This useMemo decides which code block renderer to use: fancy line-by-line ELI5 renderer if annotations exist, otherwise Docusaurus's CodeBlock, otherwise just raw children.
explanations.415: This takes the last part of a file path (like '/src/app.tsx' becomes 'app.tsx') and lowercases it for consistent matching.
explanations.419: isDockerFile checks multiple Docker filename patterns because the Dockerfile could be named 'Dockerfile', 'docker', or have various extensions.
explanations.438: variantKey picks a style variant in order: use the variant prop if given, otherwise detect Docker/Docusaurus, otherwise use the language map, otherwise 'none'.
explanations.450: styles[`variant_${variantKey}`] looks up a CSS class name by constructing the string 'variant_docker' or 'variant_python' etc., falling back to an empty string if not found.
explanations.457: displayTitle builds the heading text, preferring an explicit title, then filename, then the language name in uppercase, or 'Snippet' as last resort.
